title: 谋杀启事
summary:
During an investigation, Miss Chickering calls Miss Muggleton from an orchard to meet with the police. Miss Muggleton, appearing breathless and disheveled, asks if the officers are from Scotland Yard. Superintendent Coradine of Milchester questions Miss Muggleton, who provides an alibi stating she was admiring chrysanthemums during the crime. She describes the chaos of the event, noting she heard a voice command people to "raise your hands" and believed a refugee girl was being murdered in the dark. Miss Quincannon suggests the inspectors visit the vicar, Mrs. Harmon, noting that despite her muddled appearance, she is astute.

Inspector Coradine visits Mrs. Harmon in her shabby house. Mrs. Harmon explains that she closed her eyes during the gunshots because she dislikes being dizzy. She describes hearing various people screaming and pushing in the dark. Once the lights were lit, she saw a dead, pink-faced foreigner lying on the ground with a revolver by his side.

Later, Corrado and Ridestad review interview records. Ridestad notes that the deceased, Rudi Scherz, had a criminal record involving forgery and con artistry. Corrado expresses doubt regarding the official conclusion that the death was either an accident or a suicide, noting that the motive remains unconvincing. Corrado suspects the foreign girl may have conspired with Scherz to steal valuables, though Miss Blacklock and others claim nothing of value is in the house. Corrado also mentions that Miss Bonner believes Scherz attempted to murder Miss Blacklock, though he characterizes her as an unreliable witness whose theory is based solely on her own impressions.
